title: The Scholar's Reckoning
description: Before the palace — the Guild scholar reveals the chart is a guide to the party's own journey, written by Mordaine's hand.

steps.text: The Mage had insisted on returning to the Guild before the palace. The insistence had the quality of someone following a thread they didn't yet understand.
The scholar met them in the Guild's back room with the look of a man choosing between his safety and his need to be understood. He chose the latter.

lines.speaker: Scholar
lines.text: I found more documents. In the deepest part of the archive. The part that technically doesn't exist.

lines.speaker: Scholar
lines.text: The administrative chart I showed you — I was wrong about what it describes. It isn't a political structure.

lines.speaker: Scholar
lines.text: It's a guide. For a journey. Your journey, specifically. Every major location. Every significant enemy. Written before you arrived.

lines.speaker: Scholar
lines.text: The handwriting on the oldest documents matches the enchantments in the cave. The same as the corrupted forest seals. The same as the inscription the Arbiter guided you to.

lines.speaker: Scholar
lines.text: All of it is the same hand. The same author.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Mordaine wrote a guide to defeating her?

lines.speaker: Scholar
lines.text: Not for her defeat. For your challenge. The distinction matters and I don't yet understand it.

steps.text: They had both said it at the same time. Neither of them pointed that out.
The scholar unrolled the final document. His eyes moved across the last column. Something there — his expression shifted, became careful in the way of a man handling something fragile.

lines.speaker: Scholar
lines.text: There are things in this final column that wouldn't make sense to you yet.

steps.text: He rolled the document closed. His hands were steady. The finality in the gesture was not unkindness. It was the only honest thing he had left to give them.
